name: Chocolate Peanut Butter Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 300°F.
In a large bowl, beat together cream cheese and sugar until light and fluffy, about 2 to 3 minutes. Beat in eggs one at a time. Add vanilla, beating until smooth. Add heavy cream, beating until well blended.
Transfer 1/3 of batter to another bowl and set aside. Beat melted chocolate into remaining batter. Pour mixture into crust. With mixer on medium speed, beat peanut butter into remaining 1/3 batter until thoroughly mixed. Carefully spoon peanut butter mixture over chocolate cheese mixture in pan. Using the long handle of a wooden spoon, swirl the two flavors together.
Bake about 1 hour, or until cheesecake is set around edges and puffed but still jiggles slightly in the center. Let cake cool for 2 hours, then refrigerate until well chilled, at least 8 hours or overnight.
For a crust, I use Oreo cookie crumbs and make a crust using their recipe. I also do not swirl the flavors together. I like them layered better.
